content.full_text:
State of California                                    A-81/186

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444


October 26, 1981


George Osborne, Executive Director
Santa Ana River Flood Protection Agency
811 No. Broadway, Suite 600
Santa Ana, CA  92701

Dear Mr. Osborne:

     This is in response to your letter dated October 21, 1981, in
which you asked for information regarding your filing obligations pursuant
to lobbyist disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

     Government Code Section 82039 defines a lobbyist as "...any per-
son who is employed or contracts for economic consideration, other than
reimbursement for reasonable travel expenses, to communicate directly or
through his agents with any elective state official, agency official or
legislative official for the purpose of influencing legislative or
administrative action, if a substantial portion of the activities
for which he receives consideration is for the purpose of influencing
legislative or administrative action."

     In reviewing the information enclosed with your letter, it
appears that you are receiving compensation to communicate directly with
agency and legislative officials for the purpose of influencing legisla-
tive or administrative action. Pursuant to Government Code Section 82039,
you would therefore be required to register as a lobbyist and file quar-
terly lobbyist reports.

     I have enclosed some materials to aid you with your filing
requirements. If you need any additional material or information, please
do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Your Very Truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        Lynn Montgomery
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

Santa Ana River
Flood Protection Agency
811 North Broadway, Suite 600    Santa Ana, California • 92701    Phone (714) 834-5142


October 21, 1981


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

     I have been Executive Director of this joint powers agency
since August 31, 1981.

     In the agency files I have encountered a letter dated
January 30, 1981, from Mr. Bruce W. Mathias addressed to Mr.
Edward Just, then Executive Director of this agency.  A copy
of the letter is enclosed.

     Please review the enclosed copy of the contract for my
services and advise me of my obligations for filing and report-
ing under applicable state law.

                                        Sincerely,



                                        ________________________
                                        George Osborne
                                        Executive Director

GO:jr

Enclosures (2)

AGREEMENT

EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR SERVICES

This AGREEMENT is entered into this 31st day of AUGUST,

1981, between the SANTA ANA RIVER FLOOD PROTECTION AGENCY, hereinafter

referred to as "AGENCY" and H. G. OSBORNE, hereinafter referred to as

"CONTRACTOR."

WHEREAS, the Santa Ana River Flood Protection Agency was created

under the terms of an agreement dated December 31, 1974, for the purpose

of the exercise of the common powers of the parties, and particularly,

to do the following:

A. To disseminate information on the flood hazard potential of the

Santa Ana River and feasible plans for mitigating the flood threat;

B. To seek a position of unanimity and a statement of mutual

interest of the citizens of Orange, Riverside and San Bernardino Counties

in support of the most feasible alternative plan for flood protection

along the Santa Ana River;

C. To solicit Congressional authorization and appropriations for

works of improvement; and

WHEREAS, AGENCY has the power to make and enter into contracts and

to retain a part-time officer for the exercise of its powers; and

WHEREAS, the AGENCY desires to contract for the services of a person

who is by training and experience competent to act as AGENCY'S Executive

Director; and

WHEREAS, CONTRACTOR, a Registered Civil Engineer, possesses the

education, experience and personal characteristics necessary to provide

the desired services;

NOW, THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION of mutual promises, covenants and

conditions hereinafter set forth, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. The term of the Agreement shall be from the date recorded above

until June 30, 1982, and shall be automatically renewed on July 1 of each

Page 2

1    subsequent year unless either party gives thirty (30) days advance

2    notice of non-renewal.  In addition, the Agreement may be terminated for

3    cause by either party upon the giving of a thirty (30) day notice in

4    writing.

5         2.  CONTRACTOR's services shall be rendered under the general

6    direction and supervision of the Executive Committee of the AGENCY.

7         3.  CONTRACTOR's services shall be as an independent contractor and

8    he shall not be an employee of AGENCY or its constituent members.

9         4.  CONTRACTOR agrees to secure at his own expense all offices,

10   clerical and miscellaneous services necessary to perform the services

11   required under this Agreement.  CONTRACTOR shall exonerate, indemnify

12   and hold harmless AGENCY from and against any and all liability, claims,

13   loss, damage or expense by reason of any act or omission of any employee,

14   servant or agency of CONTRACTOR.

15        5.  AGENCY agrees that CONTRACTOR may represent other clients in

16   addition to his responsibilities as CONTRACTOR under this Agreement, but

17   the interests of AGENCY shall be superior to those of any other client.

18   Should a potential conflict of interest ever arise, CONTRACTOR shall

19   immediately notify AGENCY.  Furthermore, CONTRACTOR shall provide AGENCY

20   with the names and addresses of all clients served by written contract

21   and a summary of the scope of services provided in those contracts so

22   that AGENCY may identify any potential conflicts.  If a potential

23   conflict is identified by either party, CONTRACTOR and Executive Committee

24   shall agree on CONTRACTOR'S further role in the conflicting matter.  A

25   written copy of the Agreement shall be prepared, signed and maintained by

26   both parties.

27        6.  In consideration of CONTRACTOR's rendering the services to

28   AGENCY set forth in this Agreement, AGENCY agrees to pay to CONTRACTOR

Page 3

1 the amount of $1,930/month for such services, which shall include full

2 compensation for all services except travel outside the Los Angeles,

3 Orange, Riverside and San Bernardino County areas, and hotel expenses.

4 Said travel and hotel expenses shall be reimbursable in accordance with

5 the rules and regulations established by the County Auditor/Controller.

6 Said monthly amount of $1,930 shall be paid on or before the tenth of

7 each month, commencing October, 1981, and continue until this Agreement

8 is terminated. In the event of termination prior to the end of any

9 month, the final payment shall be prorated to reflect the number of days

10 the Agreement is in force. Said monthly amount shall be subject to

11 change by resolution of AGENCY'S Commission at the time of adoption of

12 the yearly budget and the previous year's change in the Consumer Price

13 Index shall be considered.

14 7. CONTRACTOR'S services shall consist of the following:

15 A. Set up and maintain an operative office in Santa Ana,

16 California;

17 B. Advocate the interests of AGENCY with members of Congress,

18 Federal agencies, boards, commissions, committees, and their staffs;

19 C. Advocate the interests of AGENCY with members of the state

20 legislature, state agencies, boards, commissions, committees and

21 their staffs;

22 D. Provide liaison between cities, counties of the Santa Ana

23 River watershed (Riverside, San Bernardino and Orange), agencies of

24 the State of California, and the Corps of Engineers and other

25 federal agencies involved in the processing of the Santa Ana River

26 Main Stem Project to completion;

27 E. Solicit cooperation among the local agencies of the Santa

28 Ana River watershed in the interest of securing a unified position

Page 4

1    of support for the ultimate completion of the Santa Ana River Main

2    Stem Project;

3         F.  Monitor, assist and expedite each step in the process of

4    securing completion of the Santa Ana River Main Stem Project;

5         G.  Report periodically, at least annually, in writing to the

6    agencies, commissioners, concerning the nature and extent of services

7    rendered hereunder;

8         H.  Maintain regular contacts with elected representatives,

9    their staffs, the Corps of Engineers and other federal officials, so

10   as to be aware of local, state and federal needs, desires, policies

11   and impacts relevant to the support and processing of the Santa Ana

12   River Project to completion.

13        8.  CONTRACTOR and AGENCY agree that part of the services purchased

14   by AGENCY in this Agreement include CONTRACTOR'S maintaining suitable

15   office facilities and clerical support for performing the services under

16   this Agreement.  Such office facilities shall be available for use by

17   AGENCY commissioners in the performance of their AGENCY business in

18   Santa Ana, California.

19        9.  CONTRACTOR shall procure and keep in force public liability and

20   property damage insurance satisfactory to the AGENCY covering automobiles

21   owned, operated, leased, maintained or used by the CONTRACTOR in the

22   conduct of AGENCY business.  The minimum combined coverage for public

23   liability and property damage shall be $400,000 for each accident.  Such

24   insurance policy to be for the protection of both the AGENCY and the

25   CONTRACTOR.

26        10.  CONTRACTOR shall procure and keep in force Workmen's Compensation

27   Insurance covering all persons he may employ in the course of performing

28   his obligations under this contract, and shall file with the County

1    Auditor/Controller a certificate of Workmen's Compensation Insurance,

2    issued by an admitted insurer.

3         11.  AGENCY shall not be liable for any act or acts of the CONTRACTOR

4    and nothing herein contained shall be construed as creating the relation-

5    ship of employer and employee between the parties.  CONTRACTOR shall be

6    deemed to be at all times an independent CONTRACTOR and the provisions

7    of this contract referring to the direction from the Executive Committee

8    of AGENCY shall be construed as providing for direction as to policy and

9    the result of the CONTRACTOR'S work only, and not as the means by which

10   such result is accomplished.

11        12.  This contract shall be personal and not assignable.

12        13.  This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted according to

13   the laws of the State of California.

14        14.  Any notice or notices required or permitted to be given

15   pursuant to this Agreement may be personally served on other party

16   by the party giving such notice, or may be served by certified mail,

17   postage prepaid, return receipt requested, to the following addresses:

18             AGENCY:    Chairman, Santa Ana River Flood Protection Agency
                         c/o Clerk of the Orange County Board of Supervisors
19                       10 Civic Center Plaza
                         Santa Ana, California

20        CONTRACTOR:    H. G. Osborne
21                      1225 Crestview Drive
                        Fullerton, California 92633

22

23

24

25

26

27

28

Page 6

1    IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Agreement

2    on the day and year dated below.

3
                                        SANTA ANA RIVER FLOOD PROTECTION
4                                       AGENCY, a joint powers agency

5
     Dated___Aug. 31_______,1981    By___________________
6                                       Commission Chairman

7    ATTEST:

8

9    _____________________

10

11   APPROVED AS TO FORM:

12   ADRIAN KUYPER, COUNTY COUNSEL

13   AG.WHILSTOT, ASST.

14   By_____________________

15   Dated___Aug. 26_______,1981

16

17
                                        By_____________________
18                                       H.G. OsMorne
                                        "CONTRACTOR"

sections.question_synthetic: Does George Osborne, as Executive Director of the Santa Ana River Flood Protection Agency receiving $1,930/month compensation to advocate the Agency's interests with Congressional members, federal agencies, state legislators, and state agencies, have filing obligations as a lobbyist under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: George Osborne is required to register as a lobbyist and file quarterly lobbyist reports pursuant to Government Code Section 82039, as he is receiving compensation to communicate directly with agency and legislative officials for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request from George Osborne dated October 21, 1981, followed by FPPC response letter dated October 26, 1981 from Lynn Montgomery. The response letter provides the actual advice. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but the structure and content clearly indicate the advisory nature. The contract document provided context for the determination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does George Osborne, as Executive Director of the Santa Ana River Flood Protection Agency receiving $1,930/month compensation to advocate the Agency's interests with Congressional members, federal agencies, state legislators, and state agencies, have filing obligations as a lobbyist under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: George Osborne is required to register as a lobbyist and file quarterly lobbyist reports pursuant to Government Code Section 82039, as he is receiving compensation to communicate directly with agency and legislative officials for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises George Osborne, newly appointed Executive Director of the Santa Ana River Flood Protection Agency, that his contract services involving advocacy with Congressional members, federal agencies, and state legislators constitute lobbying activities requiring registration and quarterly reporting under Government Code Section 82039.
